### Post by ajgreeny on 2015-09-16
I don't understand what you are trying to do, but it looks as if you have a live OS of Ubuntu that will not run in VBox.

How are you attempting to boot to Ubuntu; using a DVD or USB, or direct from the .iso file and where is the code that you have copied; what file is it from?

It is best in my opinion to boot a new VM using the .iso file on your hard disk without burning it to DVD or USB.

So tell us in more detail what you are really wanting, what you have done, and what Host OS you have; it looks like Windows to me.

EDIT:
I have also moved this thread to the Virtualisation forum section as it looks as if it fits better here.
